2603.23610.title: Environment Maps: Structured Environmental Representations for Long-Horizon Agents
2603.23610.paper_summary: Although large language models (LLMs) have advanced rapidly, robust automation of complex software workflows remains an open problem. In long-horizon settings, agents frequently suffer from cascading errors and environmental stochasticity; a single misstep in a dynamic interface can lead to task failure, resulting in hallucinations or trial-and-error. This paper introduces $\textit{Environment Maps}$: a persistent, agent-agnostic representation that mitigates these failures by consolidating heterogeneous evidence, such as screen recordings and execution traces, into a structured graph. The representation consists of four core components: (1) Contexts (abstracted locations), (2) Actions (parameterized affordances), (3) Workflows (observed trajectories), and (4) Tacit Knowledge (domain definitions and reusable procedures). We evaluate this framework on the WebArena benchmark across five domains. Agents equipped with environment maps achieve a 28.2% success rate, nearly doubling the performance of baselines limited to session-bound context (14.2%) and outperforming agents that have access to the raw trajectory data used to generate the environment maps (23.3%). By providing a structured interface between the model and the environment, Environment Maps establish a persistent foundation for long-horizon planning that is human-interpretable, editable, and incrementally refinable.
2603.23610.paper_summary_zh: 儘管大型語言模型（LLMs）快速進步，但複雜軟體工作流程的穩健自動化仍然是一個未解決的問題。在長期設定中，代理經常遭受連鎖錯誤和環境隨機性；在動態介面中的一次失誤可能導致任務失敗，從而產生幻覺或試錯。本論文介紹了 $\textit{Environment Maps}$：一種持久的、與代理無關的表示，通過將異質證據（如螢幕錄影和執行痕跡）整合為結構化圖形來減輕這些失敗。該表示由四個核心組成部分構成：（1）上下文（抽象位置），（2）行動（參數化的可供性），（3）工作流程（觀察到的軌跡），以及（4）隱性知識（領域定義和可重用程序）。我們在五個領域的 WebArena 基準上評估了這一框架。配備環境地圖的代理達到 28.2% 的成功率，幾乎是僅限於會話上下文的基準（14.2%）的兩倍，並且超過了可以訪問生成環境地圖所用原始軌跡數據的代理（23.3%）。通過在模型和環境之間提供結構化介面，環境地圖為長期規劃建立了一個持久的基礎，該基礎是人類可解釋的、可編輯的，並且可以逐步改進。
2603.23610.author: Yenchia Feng et.al.
2603.23610.authors: Yenchia Feng, Chirag Sharma, Karime Maamari
